evals.prompt: We sell foundation, beauty sponges, and setting spray on Shopify. We want a 'complete the look' one-click bundle so customers can add foundation + sponge + setting spray together. We don't want to discount—just convenience. Give us bundle naming, where to put it on the product page, and what to measure.
evals.expected_output: Summary (gap, bundle type, top 3 actions, metrics); bundle definition with a clear name and SKU logic; naming and copy (headline, CTA, one-line value); placement (PDP above fold, one-click add set, keep single-item option); pricing note (no discount, value = convenience); metrics (AOV, bundle attach rate, conversion). All in English, actionable for Shopify.

assertions.name: has_summary
assertions.description: Output contains a Summary section with current gap, recommended bundle type, top 3 actions, and metrics to watch

assertions.name: has_bundle_definition
assertions.description: Output defines a bundle that includes foundation + beauty sponge + setting spray with a clear name and one-line value

assertions.name: has_primary_cta
assertions.description: Output includes primary CTA wording such as 'Add complete look to cart' or 'Get the set' and secondary option to add single item only

assertions.name: has_placement
assertions.description: Output specifies PDP placement (above the fold, one-click add set) and that single-item option remains visible

assertions.name: has_metrics
assertions.description: Output includes at least AOV, bundle attach rate, and conversion as metrics to measure

assertions.name: no_discount_value_convenience
assertions.description: Output states no discount and frames value as convenience (not price savings)

evals.prompt: Our beauty store has primer, concealer, powder, and brushes. We're thinking of a 'Full Base Kit' bundle and maybe a small 10% off when they buy the set. How should we structure the bundles, what copy should we use on the PDP, and how do we track if it's working?
evals.expected_output: Summary; bundle definition (e.g. Full Base Kit = primer + concealer + powder + brush) with naming and value line; copy guidance (headline, CTA, discount line like 'Save 10% when you complete the look'); placement and one-click UX; pricing (10% off set, anchoring); metrics (AOV, bundle attach rate, conversion, margin check). English, DTC terminology.

assertions.name: has_summary
assertions.description: Output contains a Summary section with gap, bundle type, top actions, and metrics

assertions.name: has_full_base_kit_components
assertions.description: Bundle definition includes primer, concealer, powder, and brush (or brushes) as the Full Base Kit

assertions.name: has_10pct_discount_copy
assertions.description: Copy guidance includes a discount line such as 'Save 10%' or '10% off when you complete the look'

assertions.name: has_placement_and_ux
assertions.description: Output specifies placement and one-click add-set UX on PDP

assertions.name: has_metrics_and_margin
assertions.description: Output includes AOV, bundle attach rate, conversion, and margin or margin check

evals.prompt: We sell lip liner, lipstick, and lip gloss. Customers often buy one and come back for the rest. We want to offer a 'Complete Lip Look' bundle so they can add all three in one click. We use WooCommerce. What should we call it, where should the bundle appear, and what's the best way to phrase the add-to-cart for the set?
evals.expected_output: Summary; bundle definition (Complete Lip Look = liner + lipstick + gloss) with clear naming; copy (headline, CTA 'Add complete lip look to cart', secondary 'Add [item] only'); placement (PDP, one-click add set, WooCommerce-friendly); metrics (AOV, attach rate, conversion). English, applicable to WooCommerce.

assertions.name: has_complete_lip_look_bundle
assertions.description: Bundle definition includes lip liner + lipstick + lip gloss with a name like 'Complete Lip Look' or equivalent

assertions.name: has_add_complete_lip_look_cta
assertions.description: Output includes CTA phrasing for adding the full set (e.g. 'Add complete lip look to cart') and secondary option to add single item only

assertions.name: has_placement_woocommerce
assertions.description: Output specifies PDP placement and mentions WooCommerce or is applicable to WooCommerce

assertions.name: has_metrics
assertions.description: Output includes AOV, attach rate, and/or conversion as metrics
